reasoning: Since the November 4 start date, there has been a breakdown in the peace agreement around November 10-13, resulting in isolated skirmishes and 1-2 reported deaths. However, there has been no major military escalation or artillery exchange akin to the five-day conflict in July 2025. Given that a month has passed since the initial breakdown of the peace deal and violence has not spiralled, geopolitical pressure (e.g., US trade leverage) appears to be keeping the conflict from escalating into mass-casualty events. With nearly half the forecast period elapsed and a required threshold of 20 deaths, the probability of reaching the remaining 18+ deaths by mid-January is quite low. This aligns with the community forecast of 5% in late November. I estimate a 4% chance.
